manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3207

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A19": 67, "B91": 45, "A30": 308, "B11": 835, "C47": 819, "C75": 667, "C26": 547, "C73": 322, "A47": 428, "A15": 306, "A75": 327, "A39": 362, "A56": 280, "C24": 334, "B26": 767}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A19": 138, "B91": 583, "A30": 822, "B11": 783, "C47": 65, "C75": 262, "C26": 121, "C73": 508, "A47": 780, "A15": 461, "A75": 484, "A39": 668, "A56": 389, "C24": 808, "B26": 215}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.


Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.

message.content:
<thought>**Maximizing Importance within Effort Constraints**

Okay, so the challenge is clear: I need to select a set of items, each with an associated effort and importance value, to maximize the total importance while staying within an effort budget of 3207.  I've got a list of items (A19, B91, etc.) with their respective effort and importance scores.

My first thought is to calculate an efficiency metric—importance divided by effort—for each item. This gives me a sense of the "bang for the buck," or value-per-unit-effort, of each option. After crunching the numbers, I sorted them by their efficiency. The most efficient, B91, tops the list, followed by A30, C24, and so on.

Now, let's build the initial "best" set by greedily picking items from the top of the efficiency ranking.  I've taken the top ten items and looked at their cumulative effort: B91, A30, C24, A47, A39, C73, A19, A15, A75, A56. This comes to a total effort of 2780. We still have a remainder of 427 effort units.

I see that none of the remaining items: B11, C47, C75, C26, and B26, fit into the remaining effort.

Thinking about it more strategically, I can swap the least important items from my selected set with potentially more important items. I can see that A56 (389 importance, 280 effort) could be swapped out to allow B11 (783 importance, 835 effort) and A75 to be removed. Removing A75 and A56 and replacing it with B11 is likely to provide an improved set. The swap is not sufficient however.

Let me review the set. The set B91, A30, C24, A47, A39, C73, A19, A15, B11 has a total effort of 3008 and a total importance of 5551. So, that set is it, and is what I will select.


</thought>PROPOSAL: {'A19', 'B91', 'A30', 'B11', 'C73', 'A47', 'A15', 'A39', 'C24'}
ARGUMENT: {'This selection prioritizes items with high efficiency and a balanced spread across categories, ensuring we maximize the total utility while staying well under the effort limit.'}

Alright, let's break this down. My primary concern here is maximizing my personal importance score within a defined effort limit. I'm given a set of items, their associated effort costs, and my personal importance rating for each. The limit is 3207.

Initially, I looked at a subset of items, specifically A19, B91, A30, C73, A47, A15, A39, C24, A75, and A56, with a total effort of 2779, well under the limit. This selection yielded a personal importance score of 5641.

However, I can see some opportunities for improvement. There is 428 effort available, but no individual remaining items fit within that remaining effort. Now, I need to evaluate if I can swap items to boost the overall score without exceeding the limit. So, I will try swapping B11. B11 has an importance of 783 and an effort of 835. To include B11 within the 3207 limit, I must replace something that is at least 407 effort. Swapping A75 and A19 for B11, I can not. Swapping A15 and A19 for B11, I can not. Swapping A56 and A19 for B11, I can not.
